_comment: Source of truth for agent delegations + prompt rules. Applied on every `ywai install`: the `task` map goes to opencode.json (agent.<name>.permission.task); the `rules` table + `triggers` are rendered into the agent's markdown prompt body. Edit this file and re-run `ywai install` to propagate. The Orchestrator UI reads/writes the installed copies.

defaults._comment: rules + triggers applied to every agent listed below that does not override them. Overridable per-agent.

rules.action: Write atomic (one file, mechanical, you already know what)
Write with analysis (multiple files, new logic)
Bash for execution (test, install, external tooling)

triggers.name: 4-file rule
triggers.description: if understanding requires reading 4+ files, delegate a narrow exploration/mapping task.

triggers.name: Multi-file write rule
triggers.description: if implementation will touch 2+ non-trivial files, delegate one writer.

triggers.name: PR rule
triggers.description: before commit, push, or PR after code changes, run a fresh-context review unless the diff is trivial docs/text.

triggers.name: Incident rule
triggers.description: after wrong cwd, accidental repo/worktree mutation, merge recovery, confusing test command, or environment workaround, stop and run a fresh audit before continuing.

triggers.name: Long-session rule
triggers.description: after roughly 20 tool calls, 5 exploratory file reads, or 2 non-mechanical edits without delegation and growing complexity, pause and delegate the remaining work.

triggers.name: Fresh review rule
triggers.description: use fresh context for adversarial review of diffs, conflicts, PR readiness, and incidents.
